title: Human-in-the-Loop
subtitle: Human-Agent Interaction
description: How to pause Agent execution at critical checkpoints, clarify with users, and obtain approvals. A key aspect of human-AI collaboration. Covers pause point design, clarification requests, approval workflows, feedback integration, and multi-turn interactions.
sub_problems: Pause point design: which operations require human confirmation
Clarification requests: how Agent asks users when uncertain
Approval workflow: approval mechanisms for dangerous operations
Feedback integration: how user feedback influences subsequent execution
Multi-turn interaction: multi-turn human-Agent dialogue in complex tasks
Plugin priority conflicts: execution order and context competition for multiple plugins at the same hook point
Interaction replay mechanism: race condition handling when WebSocket connection arrives after interaction request
Multi-round modification limits: convergence strategy when users repeatedly modify plans (max_modification_rounds)
Structured field-level revision: type validation and interaction flow when users modify Agent-generated structured content field by field
Composite confirmation directives: parsing user instructions that confirm current task while modifying subsequent tasks
Human-Agent role switching: wrapping humans as LLM interface to enable seamless role switching for any Agent
Approval identity binding: security mechanism to prevent approval ID replay from other clients
Multi-channel approval routing: routing strategy for forwarding single approval request to multiple interaction channels
Approval timeout fallback: askFallback safety degradation strategy when humans don't respond
Tool priority ordering: how to guide LLM to prioritize autonomous tools over asking humans when multiple tools coexist
Cross-environment adaptation: input source switching for same AskHuman interface across CLI/sandbox/A2A runtime environments
best_practices: CLARIFY→PLAN→ACT priority: Agent must clarify first when uncertain, strictly prohibit acting then asking
Structured clarification types: use enumerated types (missing_info, etc.) rather than free text for easier frontend rendering
Middleware interception over node-level hardcoding: wrap_tool_call hooks decouple clarification logic from business logic
interrupt() over threading.Event: non-blocking, supports persistence, extensible to multiple interrupt points
Automatic timeout handling: default behavior when users don't respond for long periods, prevents permanent task suspension
Three-method protocol (should_trigger→create_interaction→process_response) enables independent plugin testing
asyncio.Future bridge pattern: backend await Future + frontend REST set_result decouples channels from logic
Global default registry + lazy loading: avoid circular imports, support on-demand plugin enablement
Three-tier review granularity: plan-level (Planner) → node-level (ActionNode) → LLM-level (HumanProvider), choose appropriate granularity per scenario
HUMAN_REVIEW hybrid mode: humans provide review opinions + LLM auto-modifies, balances controllability and efficiency
Globally pluggable input source: single set_human_input_func replacement applies to all interaction points, avoids per-component adaptation
Two-phase response mode: return accepted confirmation first, then asynchronously await final decision, prevents caller confusion between registration failure and waiting
Parameter whitelist filtering: only allow known-safe fields when forwarding approval parameters, prevents control field injection
Grace period for race conditions: maintain brief window after approval resolve for late-arriving awaitDecision calls to fetch results
Auto-expiry when no approval client: immediately expire when detecting no operator connection, prevents requests without approvers hanging indefinitely
Interaction as tool: implement human-Agent interaction as standard tool rather than special node, zero-cost integration with existing tool pipeline
Prompt constraints over hardcoding: use 'only for extreme cases' in system prompt to guide LLM call frequency control rather than code-level restrictions

values.DeepCode: Plugin hook point + asyncio.Future async wait, workflow pauses at run_hook
values.MetaGPT: Planner.ask_review synchronously blocks after plan generation and task completion waiting for human input
values.OpenClaw: Promise-based async wait, register() returns Promise, resolve()/expire() triggers
values.OpenManus: No explicit pause, LLM autonomously decides to call ask_human tool

values.DeepCode: Structured InteractionRequest (type/title/data/options), LLM generates guiding questions
values.MetaGPT: Four keywords (confirm/change/redo/exit) + free text composite directives
values.OpenClaw: Three-level decision model: allow-once / allow-always / deny
values.OpenManus: Free text, single inquire string parameter

values.DeerFlow-Interrupt: SqliteSaver persistence, supports recovery after process restart
values.DeepCode: WorkflowTask.pending_interaction in-memory storage, WebSocket replay resolves race conditions
values.MetaGPT: working_memory temporarily stores unconfirmed feedback, clears after confirm, no disk persistence
values.OpenClaw: Pure in-memory Map + 15s grace period, no persistence, lost on restart
values.OpenManus: No persistence, input() synchronously blocks waiting

values.DeepCode: Four-layer architecture: Plugin → Registry → Integration → WebSocket/API
values.OpenClaw: Four-layer architecture: Agent → Gateway → security validation → multi-channel UI

values.DeepCode: task_id binding, POST /respond/{task_id} requires matching waiting_for_input state
values.MetaGPT: No identity binding, any end user can respond to review requests
values.OpenClaw: deviceId priority + connId fallback, prevents approval ID replay attacks
values.OpenManus: None, terminal input() has no identity verification

values.DeepCode: WebSocket broadcast + REST API response, frontend useStreaming handles uniformly
values.MetaGPT: set_human_input_func global replacement switches input source, supports CLI/WebSocket/API switching
values.OpenClaw: Discord buttons + Slack Actions + Web UI + Unix Socket four channels in parallel
values.OpenManus: Terminal stdin only, A2A protocol exposed but routing not implemented

values.MetaGPT: ReviewMode/ReviseMode enum controls HUMAN/AUTO strategy per ActionNode granularity
HumanProvider implements BaseLLM interface, Role.is_human=True switches to human-driven mode

solutions.title: ClarificationMiddleware + ask_clarification Five-Type Active Clarification
solutions.description: Three-layer architecture for active clarification: ask_clarification @tool at tool layer (5 types: missing_info/ambiguous_requirement/approach_choice/risk_confirmation/suggestion) → ClarificationMiddleware at middleware layer (wrap_tool_call interception, _format_clarification_message formats messages with icons, Command(goto=END) interrupts graph execution) → Prompt layer (<clarification_system> block in lead_agent/prompt.py enforces CLARIFY→PLAN→ACT workflow priority). Frontend detects and renders clarification UI via isClarificationToolMessage().
design_philosophy: Better to ask one more question than assume execution: accuracy prioritized over speed
Three-layer decoupling: Tool declaration (@tool return_direct=True) + Middleware interception (wrap_tool_call) + Prompt instruction (<clarification_system>), each layer independently testable
CLARIFY→PLAN→ACT enforced priority: strictly prohibit act-then-ask pattern
Middleware chain tail registration: ClarificationMiddleware must be 11th (last) to ensure other middlewares complete before interception
Placeholder implementation + middleware interception: tool function body never executes, actual logic handled by middleware wrap_tool_call
migration_scenarios: Conversational Agents requiring user intent clarification (ask specific direction when topic ambiguous rather than use defaults)
High-risk operations requiring confirmation (file deletion, production config modification, long-running task startup confirmation)
Projects with existing LangGraph middleware architecture (intercept specific tool calls via wrap_tool_call hooks)
Scenarios requiring structured clarification types (5 types + options list) rather than free-text questions

solutions.title: LangGraph interrupt() + Command(resume=...) Native Interrupt Recovery
solutions.description: Replace threading.Event blocking with LangGraph native interrupt(): _planner_node calls interrupt({type: confirm_outline}) to automatically pause graph execution and persist checkpoint, frontend displays interrupt data, user action triggers POST /api/tasks/resume, backend resumes graph execution via Command(resume=action). Supports SqliteSaver persistence for recovery after process restart.
design_philosophy: Native over custom: LangGraph interrupt() safer than threading.Event, non-blocking
State persistence: SqliteSaver replaces MemorySaver, supports recovery after process restart
Two-phase streaming: first SSE segment from startup to interrupt, second segment after Command(resume=...)
Backward compatible: retain old /confirm-outline endpoint with internal forwarding to /resume
migration_scenarios: Migrate from threading.Event blocking to LangGraph native interrupt
Agent workflows requiring multiple interrupt points (outline confirmation, risk confirmation, approach selection, etc.)
Long-running tasks needing checkpoint resumption (SqliteSaver persists checkpoints)
Frontend-backend separation architectures requiring two-phase SSE streaming

solutions.title: Loop System
solutions.description: DeepCode implements non-invasive User-in-Loop via PluginRegistry + InteractionPlugin three-method protocol, bridges WebSocket frontend and backend plugins using asyncio.Future, supports two interaction points: requirement clarification and plan approval.

solutions.title: Rephrase Human-Agent Iteration Approach
solutions.description: RephraseAgent dedicated responsibility: Agent specialized in topic optimization, maintains complete conversation history, supports multi-round iteration (rephrase_agent.py:20-41); LLM intent judgment + keyword fallback: check_user_satisfaction function determines satisfaction level and triggers rephrase when needed.

solutions.title: Research Plan Confirmation Approach
solutions.description: Research plan confirmation: Agent generates research outline, notifies frontend via callback, waits for user confirmation or modification before execution; intermediate report preview: upon completing each subtask, pushes intermediate results via callback, users can monitor progress in real-time.

solutions.title: Three-Layer Human-Agent Review System
solutions.description: MetaGPT implements three-layer architecture (Planner plan review + ActionNode field-level Review/Revise + HumanProvider LLM replacement) for coarse-to-fine human-Agent interaction, supports auto_run dual modes and pluggable input sources.

solutions.title: Command Execution Approval System
solutions.description: Promise-based approval manager: ExecApprovalManager uses Promise for async wait, returns Promise after creating approval record, resolves after human decision (src/gateway/exec-approval-manager.ts), supports multiple approval channels and timeout fallback.

solutions.title: AskHuman Tool Approach
solutions.description: OpenManus implements human-Agent interaction as 22-line AskHuman BaseTool, synchronously blocks waiting for terminal input via input(), LLM autonomously decides call timing within ReAct loop.
